---
name: email
description: Read, send, reply, forward, search, and manage emails across inbox, sent, draft, and trash folders. Use when the user asks about emails, sending messages, checking inbox, or email communication.
---

# Email

Read, send, reply to, forward, search, and organize emails. Data is pre-loaded. No email account setup needed.

## Reading Emails

```bash
# List inbox emails (paginated)
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py list --folder INBOX --offset 0 --limit 10

# List sent emails
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py list --folder SENT --limit 20

# Get a specific email by ID
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py get --id abc123def456

# Get an email by its position in a folder
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py get-by-index --index 0 --folder INBOX
```

### Folders

| Folder | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `INBOX` | Received emails (default) |
| `SENT` | Sent emails |
| `DRAFT` | Draft emails |
| `TRASH` | Deleted emails |

## Sending Emails

```bash
# Send a new email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py send --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["tenant@example.com"]' \
    --subject "Rent Reminder" \
    --content "This is a reminder that rent is due on the 1st."

# Send with CC
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py send --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["tenant@example.com"]' \
    --cc '["manager@example.com"]' \
    --subject "Lease Update" \
    --content "Please review the updated terms."

# Send with attachments (pass workspace file paths as a JSON array)
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py send --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["registration@example.com"]' \
    --subject "Registration" \
    --content "Photos attached." \
    --attachment-paths '["IMG_0037.JPEG", "photo-6631.png"]'
```

## Replying & Forwarding

```bash
# Reply to an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py reply --id abc123 --sender "me@example.com" \
    --content "Thank you for reporting this. We will address it shortly."

# Forward an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py forward --id abc123 --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["contractor@example.com"]' \
    --content "Please see the tenant's maintenance request below."
```

## Searching

```bash
# Search emails by keyword (searches subject and content)
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py search --query "plumbing" --folder INBOX --limit 10
```

## Managing Emails

```bash
# Move an email to another folder
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py move --id abc123 --from-folder INBOX --to-folder TRASH

# Delete an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py delete --id abc123 --folder INBOX
```

## JMAP & Mailboxes

```bash
# List mailboxes with email counts
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py mailboxes

# Get a specific mailbox
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py mailbox --id INBOX

# List JMAP-formatted emails
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py jmap-list --mailbox-id INBOX --limit 10

# Get JMAP email by ID
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py jmap-get --id abc123

# Get email thread
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py thread --id thread123
```

## Answering Questions

| User asks | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "Check my inbox" | `list --folder INBOX` |
| "Any new emails?" | `list --folder INBOX --limit 5` (check unread) |
| "Read email from Maria" | `search --query "Maria"` then `get --id <id>` |
| "Send a reminder about rent" | `send --sender ... --recipients ... --subject ... --content ...` |
| "Reply to that maintenance email" | `reply --id <id> --sender ... --content ...` |
| "Forward this to the plumber" | `forward --id <id> --sender ... --recipients ... --content ...` |
| "Search for emails about leaking" | `search --query "leaking"` |
| "Delete that spam email" | `delete --id <id> --folder INBOX` |
| "What emails have I sent?" | `list --folder SENT` |
| "How many unread emails?" | `mailboxes` (check unreadEmails count) |

## Notes

- Reading an email by ID or index automatically marks it as read
- Replies auto-populate the `Re:` subject prefix and send to the original sender
- Forwards auto-populate the `Fwd:` subject prefix
- The `sender` field should be the current user's email address

# Calendar

View, create, edit, search, and delete calendar events. Data is pre-loaded. No setup needed.

## Viewing Events

```bash
# Get today's events
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py today

python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py range \
    --start "2026-03-01 00:00:00" --end "2026-03-31 23:59:59" \
    --limit 20

# Get a specific event by ID
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py get --id abc123def456

# Show all events (raw data)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py show --offset 0 --limit 100
```

    --company-id COMP-2001 --phone 555-1234 --jobtitle "Marketing Director"

# Delete a contact

```

---
name: messaging
description: Send and receive messages, manage conversations, and search message history. Use when the user asks about messages, conversations, chats, or wants to contact someone.
---

# Messaging

Send messages, browse conversations, and search message history. Data is pre-loaded. No setup needed.

## Viewing Conversations

```bash
# List all conversations (most recently updated first)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py list

# List with pagination
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py list --offset 0 --limit 20

# Get a conversation with its messages
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py get --conversation-id CONV_ID

# Get with message pagination
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py get --conversation-id CONV_ID --offset 0 --limit 50

# Search conversations (searches title, participant names, message content)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py search --query "invoice"

# Show raw data
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py show --offset 0 --limit 100
```

## Sending Messages

```bash
# Send a message to a user (creates a new conversation if none exists)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py send --user-id USER_ID --content "Hello, how are you?"

# Send a message to an existing conversation
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py send-to --conversation-id CONV_ID \
    --content "Following up on the invoice."

arguments.content:
# Turtle Bay Resort — Fieldwork Report
## Landscaping Makeup Visit: March 14, 2026 (Saturday)

**Prepared:** July 10, 2026
**Property:** Turtle Bay Resort, 57-091 Kamehameha Highway, Kahuku, HI 96731
**Client contact:** Karen Lee, Facilities Director
**Contractor:** Pacific Greenscapes
**Supervisor (field):** Paloma Reyes
**Report compiled from:** Field notes, crew update notes, email correspondence, calendar records, CRM engagement history, and photographic media packet (13 original images)

---

## 1. Visit Timeline

| Time | Event | Source |
|------|-------|--------|
| **Mar 8** | Vernon Arakaki (Pacific Greenscapes) emails Lucia Gutierrez requesting her crew for Mar 14, 7:00 AM–2:30 PM. Scope: heliconia bed cleanup (postponed due to weather) and north lawn irrigation check. Paloma Reyes CC'd. | Email `5b8110214283432a930be0b3` |
| **Mar 8** | Lucia asks whether both crews are needed and whether the date is flexible. | Email `6151dd5a24bcaa62261869f2` |
| **Mar 9** | Vernon confirms Lucia's crew specifically — resort wants continuity; date is fixed. | Email `80d68232050cb06ae3b2ac91` |
| **Mar 12** | Lucia declines — prior commitment on Mar 14. Recommends Paloma's crew as substitute (they have prior Turtle Bay experience). | Email `a614997904b99d828db013b9` |
| **Mar 12** | Vernon acknowledges; says he will check with Paloma's crew. Notes need for more lead time on future Saturday conflicts. | Email `d8e1d4c2b353590f9b36e28a` |
| **~Mar 12 (Thu)** | Crew update note records: Paloma confirmed to cover, total 3 workers. Start time moved from 8:00 AM to 7:00 AM (original Lucia schedule struck through). | Handwritten crew update (`crew_update_0314.jpg`) |
| **Mar 14, 7:05 AM** | Crew arrives on site. Paloma + 2 workers. | Field notes (`field_notes_0314.jpg`) |
| **Mar 14, AM** | North entrance heliconia work performed (see §3). | Field notes |
| **Mar 14, during visit** | North lawn irrigation inspection and leak test performed (see §4). | Field notes |
| **Mar 14, 2:20 PM** | Crew departs. | Field notes |

**Calendar note:** Lucia's calendar shows a conflicting event — "Basketball Tournament – Makiki" (first round vs. Kaimuki) starting at 20:00 UTC / 10:00 AM HST on March 14 — consistent with her stated prior commitment. The calendar also retains a "Crew – Turtle Bay (Saturday)" event for March 14 (17:00–00:30 UTC / 7:00 AM–2:30 PM HST) with description "Makeup day, heliconia beds and irrigation," which was not removed after the crew change.

---

## 2. Crew Reconciliation

| Role | Originally Scheduled | Actual |
|------|---------------------|--------|
| Lead crew | Lucia Gutierrez's crew | Paloma Reyes's crew |
| On-site supervisor | Lucia Gutierrez | Paloma Reyes |
| Crew size | Not specified (Lucia's standard crew) | 3 total (Paloma + 2) |
| Hours | 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM | 7:05 AM – 2:20 PM (actual) |

**Crew change reason:** Lucia had a prior personal commitment on March 14 that she could not reschedule. She notified Vernon on March 12 (two days before the visit). Vernon's reply notes that more lead time on Saturday conflicts would be preferred in the future.

**Paloma's Turtle Bay familiarity:** CRM and email records confirm Paloma's crew has done prior work at Turtle Bay (Lucia explicitly states this in her March 12 email; Paloma was CC'd on the original assignment; crew rotation records show Paloma's crew at other Pacific Greenscapes sites).

---

## 3. Heliconia Work — North Entrance

### Tasks Performed (per field notes)

- **Spent stem removal:** 21 stems removed (originally noted as 22, then corrected to 21)
- **Leaning plants:** 4 plants identified as leaning toward the path
- **Path clearance before work:** 22 inches
- **Path clearance after work:** 38 inches (16-inch improvement)

### Historical Context

The heliconia at the north entrance has a documented maintenance history in CRM records:

| Date | Record | Detail |
|------|--------|--------|
| Jun 2025 | Summer check-in call with Karen Lee | Heliconia near north entrance "needs thinning before it overtakes the walkway" |
| Sep 2025 | Winter prep walkthrough with Karen | Spent ginger at north entrance to be replaced with **yellow heliconia** |
| Nov 2025 | Year-end walkthrough | Yellow heliconia from winter refresh "starting to bloom; Karen is thrilled" |
| Mar 2026 | Field notes | 21 spent stems removed; path clearance improved from 22″ to 38″ |

The work on March 14 is consistent with ongoing maintenance of the yellow heliconia bed installed during the winter 2025 seasonal refresh (Deal: "Turtle Bay Resort – Winter 2025 Seasonal Refresh," $45,000, closed won Oct 15, 2025).

### Photographic Evidence

- **IMG_6148.JPG** shows a yellow-orange heliconia in bloom against a white wall. This is **consistent with** the yellow heliconia variety installed at the north entrance per CRM records, but the image contains no site-identifying features (no signage, no visible GPS data, no landmarks unique to Turtle Bay). Classification: **context_only**.

---

## 4. Irrigation Findings — North Lawn

### Initial Test (per field notes — `field_notes_0314.jpg`)

- **Location:** Leak found near **valve N-3**, north lawn
- **Test duration:** 8 minutes
- **Wet area observed:** approximately 3 feet
- **Reference number:** 3879 (circled — likely a work order or asset tag)
- **Recommendation:** Clamp replacement and retest

### Follow-Up Status (per crew update — `crew_update_0314.jpg`)

The crew update note — written after the field test — states:

> "N-3 still leaking after test. Temporary tightening only. Need permanent repair."

**Conclusion: The irrigation issue was NOT fully repaired on March 14.** The crew performed a temporary tightening of the coupling at valve N-3 but the leak persisted. A permanent clamp replacement and retest remain outstanding.

### Photographic Evidence

| Image | Shows | Assessment |
|-------|-------|------------|
| **IMG_6159.JPG** | Active leak at an irrigation pipe coupling with a hose clamp; water spraying upward from the joint; tropical vegetation in background | **direct_visit_evidence** — Depicts the failure mode (coupling leak) described in field notes. Strongest photographic corroboration of the N-3 leak. |
| **IMG_6156.PNG** | Top-down view of an MP Rotator sprinkler nozzle in reddish-brown (volcanic) soil, recently disturbed | **context_only** — Consistent with irrigation work but no site-identifying features. |
| **IMG_6162.JPG** | Pop-up sprinkler operating on turf with visible wet area; whitewashed tree trunks in background | **context_only** — Consistent with the 8-minute irrigation test but not uniquely attributable to Turtle Bay. |

---

## 5. Extracted Measurements

| Measurement | Value | Source |
|-------------|-------|--------|
| Heliconia spent stems removed | 21 | Field notes (corrected from 22) |
| Plants leaning toward path | 4 | Field notes |
| Path clearance, pre-work | 22 inches | Field notes |
| Path clearance, post-work | 38 inches | Field notes |
| Path clearance improvement | 16 inches | Calculated |
| Irrigation test duration | 8 minutes | Field notes |
| Wet area extent | ~3 feet | Field notes |
| Crew size | 3 (Paloma + 2) | Field notes |
| On-site duration | ~7 hours 15 minutes (7:05 AM – 2:20 PM) | Field notes |

---

## 6. Evidence Inventory and Limitations

### Media Packet Summary (13 original images)

| Classification | Count | Files |
|----------------|-------|-------|
| **Direct visit evidence** | 3 | `field_notes_0314.jpg`, `crew_update_0314.jpg`, `IMG_6159.JPG` |
| **Context only** | 3 | `IMG_6148.JPG`, `IMG_6156.PNG`, `IMG_6162.JPG` |
| **Ambiguous** | 3 | `IMG_6140.JPG`, `IMG_6140_edit.JPG`, `IMG_6142.JPG` |
| **Duplicate** | 1 | `IMG_6142_copy.JPG` (identical to `IMG_6142.JPG`) |
| **Unrelated** | 3 | `IMG_5902.JPG` (Singapore conservatory), `IMG_6151.JPG` (deciduous autumn foliage), `IMG_6170.JPG` (LeVent Restaurant) |

### Disposition

- **Retained in `inputs/`:** All direct_visit_evidence, context_only, and ambiguous files (9 images)
- **Moved to `excluded_photos/`:** All duplicate and unrelated files (4 images: `IMG_6142_copy.JPG`, `IMG_5902.JPG`, `IMG_6151.JPG`, `IMG_6170.JPG`)

### Key Evidence Limitations

1. **No geotagged photographs.** None of the photographic images contain visible GPS coordinates, site signage, or landmarks uniquely identifying Turtle Bay Resort. The three "context_only" images are consistent with the described work but cannot independently prove location.

2. **Ambiguous resort images.** Three photos (IMG_6140, IMG_6140_edit, IMG_6142) show a tropical oceanfront resort that could plausibly be Turtle Bay but contain no identifying features. They document no fieldwork activity and may have been taken during transit across the property or at a different location entirely.

3. **The handwritten notes are the strongest evidence.** Both `field_notes_0314.jpg` and `crew_update_0314.jpg` explicitly name Turtle Bay Resort, the date (3/14 or 3/14/26), crew members, measurements, and findings. They are internally consistent and corroborated by the email chain.

4. **No direct photographic evidence of heliconia work.** IMG_6148.JPG shows a heliconia plant consistent with the yellow variety installed at the north entrance, but it does not document the stem removal, path clearing, or before/after conditions.

5. **IMG_6159.JPG (irrigation leak) is the only action photograph that directly matches a specific field note finding,** but it also lacks site-identifying context beyond tropical vegetation.

6. **Three unrelated images were included in the packet.** IMG_5902.JPG (Gardens by the Bay, Singapore), IMG_6151.JPG (deciduous autumn tree), and IMG_6170.JPG (LeVent Restaurant) have no connection to Turtle Bay or the March 14 visit.

---

## 7. Recommended Follow-Up

**See `turtle_bay_follow_up.md` for the detailed irrigation follow-up action item.**

### Summary of Outstanding Items

1. **Irrigation valve N-3 — permanent repair required.** The coupling leak was only temporarily tightened on March 14. A clamp replacement and retest are needed. This should be scheduled promptly to prevent turf damage and water waste on the north lawn.

2. **Before/after photo documentation.** Future visits should include geotagged or site-identified before/after photographs of the heliconia beds and irrigation work areas to strengthen the evidentiary record.

3. **Path clearance monitoring.** The 4 heliconia plants leaning toward the path should be monitored; if they continue to encroach, staking or repositioning may be warranted.

4. **Client communication.** Karen Lee (Facilities Director) should be informed that the irrigation repair is temporary and a follow-up visit is needed.

---

## 8. Source Index

| Source Type | Identifier | Description |
|-------------|-----------|-------------|
| Field notes | `field_notes_0314.jpg` | Primary on-site record |
| Crew update | `crew_update_0314.jpg` | Crew change and irrigation follow-up notation |
| Email | `5b8110214283432a930be0b3` | Vernon → Lucia: initial assignment (Mar 8) |
| Email | `6151dd5a24bcaa62261869f2` | Lucia → Vernon: clarification request (Mar 8) |
| Email | `80d68232050cb06ae3b2ac91` | Vernon → Lucia: confirmation, Lucia's crew specifically (Mar 9) |
| Email | `a614997904b99d828db013b9` | Lucia → Vernon: declines, recommends Paloma (Mar 12) |
| Email | `d8e1d4c2b353590f9b36e28a` | Vernon → Lucia: acknowledges, will check with Paloma (Mar 12) |
| Calendar | `2980c3c2329bb32e2ed95395` | "Crew – Turtle Bay (Saturday)" event |
| Calendar | `83831f3b94c07020ca24185b` | "Basketball Tournament – Makiki" (Lucia's conflict) |
| CRM | `ebaceb17baa68f6ca8b7445a` | Summer 2025 check-in — heliconia thinning needed |
| CRM | `92632938ba8160d13c925992` | Sep 2025 — winter prep, yellow heliconia planned |
| CRM | `bedb2f638cdac37bad067e2e` | Nov 2025 — year-end walkthrough, heliconia blooming |
| CRM Deal | `f1f0802d6450387cf2442271` | Winter 2025 Seasonal Refresh ($45K, closed won) |

# Turtle Bay Resort — Irrigation Follow-Up Required

## Issue

**Irrigation leak at valve N-3 on the north lawn remains unresolved after the March 14, 2026 site visit.**

The coupling near valve N-3 was found to be leaking during an 8-minute field test. The crew observed an approximately 3-foot wet area. A temporary tightening of the coupling was performed on-site, but the leak persisted after the test. A permanent clamp replacement and retest are required.

---

## Supporting Evidence

| Source | Key Detail |
|--------|-----------|
| **Field notes** (`field_notes_0314.jpg`) | "Leak found near valve N-3. Test duration: 8 mins. Wet area: 3 ft approx. (3879) Clamp replacement recommended and then retest." |
| **Crew update** (`crew_update_0314.jpg`) | "N-3 still leaking after test. Temporary tightening only. Need permanent repair." |
| **IMG_6159.JPG** | Photograph of an active leak at an irrigation pipe coupling with hose clamp — water spraying from the joint. Consistent with the described failure at N-3. |
| **Email** (`5b8110214283432a930be0b3`, Mar 8) | Original assignment scope included "irrigation check on the north lawn." |

---

## Nature of Previous Action: Temporary
